item.title: Identity enrichment — AGENTS.md standard operating procedures and runbook compilation
item.intent: Every generated AGENTS.md must include standard operating procedures that make agents reliable: session startup ritual, memory discipline, data freshness rules, overnight batching, and cron role mapping. Runbooks defined in blueprints must compile into workspace identity files.
item.description: Gap analysis showed the managed AGENTS.md is 2.4KB of minimal content. The manual prototype's is 15KB with procedures that every agent benefits from. These aren't personal — they're operational discipline extracted from production experience. Additionally, the blueprint schema already has a runbooks field but it never compiles to actual workspace files.
steps: Enrich generateAgents() in src/design/identity/agents.ts with standard operating procedures section
Add session startup ritual: read SOUL.md → USER.md → daily memory → (main session only) MEMORY.md. Order matters for context priming.
Add memory discipline: daily logs in memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md (raw), MEMORY.md (curated long-term), USER.md (static facts). Write it down or lose it.
Add data freshness rules: verify cached data before citing, stale data worse than no data, check tool output timestamps

Add overnight batching rule: when quiet_hours are configured, batch findings into files, deliver ONE digest at morning_brief time. Never spam overnight unless emergency (circuit breaker, critical alert, system failure).
Add cron role mapping: auto-generate a table from blueprint.cron_config showing which job does what, when it runs, which model it uses, and what delivery mode
Scale content by blueprint complexity: minimal blueprint (no cron, low autonomy) gets startup + memory discipline only. Full blueprint gets everything.
Implement runbook compilation: blueprint.runbooks[] → workspace/identity/*.md files with token budget enforcement
Add runbook token budget: each runbook file counts against bootstrapMaxChars aggregate, warn if total exceeds limit
Update TOOLS.md generator to include per-tool quirks from integration registry (not just names)
Add unit tests for each SOP section and runbook compilation
acceptanceCriteria: Generated AGENTS.md includes session startup ritual with correct read order
Generated AGENTS.md includes memory discipline section
Generated AGENTS.md includes data freshness rules
Generated AGENTS.md includes overnight batching when quiet_hours configured
Generated AGENTS.md includes cron role mapping table when cron_config has jobs
Content scales: simple blueprints get 2-3 sections, complex blueprints get all 5
Blueprint runbooks compile to workspace/identity/*.md files
Total identity file size stays within bootstrapMaxChars (20,000 default)
TOOLS.md includes tool-specific quirks, not just names
